docs.title: Admin command overview
Owner command overview
docs.text:
addowner

Promotes a user as an bot owner.

Usage: <prefix>addowner @username
Parameter Description prefix Your channel specific prefix (Default: kok!) username The name of the user (Case-Insensitive, Can contain the @ character)

Possible errors

  • Message sender is not an owner
  • No username specified
  • Username doesn't match with regex ^(?!_)\w+$
  • User not found by Twitch api
  • User already promoted
delowner 
Alias: deleteowner

Demotes a bot owner.

Usages:
<prefix>delowner @username
<prefix>deleteowner @username
Parameter Description prefix Your channel specific prefix (Default: kok!) username The name of the user (Case-Insensitive, Can contain the @ character)

Possible errors

  • Message sender is not an owner
  • No username specified
  • User is not an owner
addadmin

Promotes a user as an bot admin.

Usage: <prefix>addadmin @username
Parameter Description prefix Your channel specific prefix (Default: kok!) username The name of the user (Case-Insensitive, Can contain the @ character)

Possible errors

  • Message sender is not an owner
  • No username specified
  • Username doesn't match with regex ^(?!_)\w+$
  • User not found by Twitch api
  • User already promoted
deladmin 
Alias: deleteadmin

Demotes a bot admin.

Usages:
<prefix>deladmin @username
<prefix>deleteadmin @username
Parameter Description prefix Your channel specific prefix (Default: kok!) username The name of the user (Case-Insensitive, Can contain the @ character)

Possible errors

  • Message sender is not an owner
  • No username specified
  • Specified user is not even an admin
  • Specified user is an owner
exit

Closes database connection, disconnects from every Twitch websocket and exits the System

Usages: <prefix>exit
Parameter Description prefix Your channel specific prefix (Default: kok!)

Possible errors

  • Message sender is not an owner
sql

Executes sql commands in the connected database and sends database metadata.

Usages: <prefix>sql <SQL-code>
Parameter Description prefix Your channel specific prefix (Default: kok!) SQL-Code The sql code to execute.

Possible errors

  • Message sender is not an owner
  • No sql code specified

docs.title: Utility command overview
docs.text:
join

Lets the bot join a specific chat. If optional parameter user is specified, you need to make sure to be mod or broadcaster at this chat. If you aren't mod or broadcaster at this chat, you need to have bot admin/owner permissions.

Usage: <prefix>join @username?
Parameter Description prefix Your channel specific prefix (Default: kok!) username The name of the user (Optional, Case-Insensitive, Can contain the @ character)

Possible errors

  • Username specified, where sender isn't even a mod or broadcaster at, without having admin/owner permissions
  • Chat already joined
  • Username doesn't match with regex ^(?!_)\w+$
  • User not found by Twitch api
part

Lets the bot leave from a specific chat. If optional parameter user is specified, you need to make sure to be mod or broadcaster at this chat. If you aren't mod or broadcaster at this chat, you need to have bot admin/owner permissions.

Usage: <prefix>part @username?
Parameter Description prefix Your channel specific prefix (Default: kok!) username The name of the user (Optional, Case-Insensitive, Can contain the @ character)

Possible errors

  • Username specified, where sender isn't even a mod or broadcaster at, without having admin/owner permissions
  • Bot not connected to chat
  • Username does match with an owner name

docs.title: Add bot to Twitch chat
docs.text:
To add the bot you need to use the kok!join command in a chat the bot is in. If the bot isn't in a chat you are in, you need to join the bot's chat and type in the command. If you want to add the bot in another chat you need also to add the username of that chat.

Tip

You can get the channel specific prefix from the bot, by simply pinging the bot in the chat with prefix in the same message. (Message must match following RegEx: ^@?apujar,? prefix(.*)?$)

For Example:

kok!join blockydotjar

Permissions needed

You need to be mod or broadcaster (if the chat is equal to your chat) at the chat you specified.

If you want to know more about this command you can click here to get to the command overview. If everything went right, the bot will response as following in the specified chat:

docs.title: Part bot from Twitch chat
docs.text:
To remove the bot you need to use the kok!part command in the chat, you want to remove the bot in. If you want to remove the bot outside of the chat, you want the bot to be removed from, you need also to add the username of that chat.

Tip

You can get the channel specific prefix from the bot, by simply pinging the bot in the chat with prefix in the same message. (Message must match following RegEx: ^@?apujar,? prefix(.*)?$)

For Example:

kok!part blockydotjar

Permissions needed

You need to be mod or broadcaster (if the chat is equal to your chat) at the chat you specified.

If you want to know more about this command you can click here to get to the command overview.

If you want to check, if everything went right you can try to execute the ping command. If the bot doesn't respond to the command, he successfully left.
